title: 'This is not a normal feeling': Jessie Buckley wins Golden Globe for Hamnet
text: Kerry-born actress Jessie Buckley claimed the best actress in a drama award at the Golden Globes for her role as Agnes Shakespeare in Chloé Zhao's film adaptation of Maggie O'Farrell's novel Hamnet. In her acceptance speech, Buckley expressed her gratitude to the organization, saying: This is not a normal feeling or situation to be in, but thank you, Golden Globes. She highlighted the international collaboration behind the production, mentioning that despite telling the story of probably the most famous Brit that ever lived, the crew included a Chinese director, many Irish team members, and predominantly Polish crew workers. The film also secured the best drama feature award, edging out Ryan Coogler's Sinners. However, co-star Paul Mescal did not win in the best supporting actor category, losing to veteran Stellan Skarsgård for Sentimental Value. Buckley is now considered a strong favorite for the Academy Award for best actress when Oscar nominations are announced on January 22nd, though she will face competition from rising performer Rose Byrne.
